Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

design: add dark/light theme system with next-themes

Install next-themes, add ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle, define CSS
design tokens for both modes in globals.css, migrate ~1500 hardcoded
colors to semantic tokens across all components. Sidebar is light in
light mode, dark navy in dark mode. All color-coded elements (badges,
file types, status indicators) use dark: variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

commit.message:
fix(qr): fix stale Redis auth not being cleared + improve /qr flow

Root cause: _deleteSessionFilesCompletely() only cleared Redis auth via
this._clearAuth, which is null when session was never initialized on the
current pod. Stale credentials in Redis caused Baileys to reconnect
instead of generating QR, resulting in perpetual 404.

Changes:
- _deleteSessionFilesCompletely: fallback to _clearRedisAuth() when
  _clearAuth is not available (session never initialized)
- /qr route: wait 8s for Baileys to generate QR naturally, then trigger
  refreshQR in background only if QR still unavailable (stale auth case).
  Frontend polls every 5s and picks up the QR on next attempt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve duplicate inscription error UX

- Backend: return friendly French message for duplicate licence constraint
- Frontend: show inline warning in modal instead of raw alert()
- Clear error message when reopening the modal

commit.message:
Add hreflang tags for bilingual EN/ES support across all pages

All public pages now emit hreflang link tags for en, es, and x-default.
Since the app serves both languages from the same URL via client-side
toggle, all hreflang alternates point to the same canonical — this tells
search engines the page serves multiple languages and prevents duplicate
content flags. Also adds og:locale:alternate for es_MX.

commit.message:
docs(resolve): 📝 add detailed Task 6 name resolution spec (#72)

## Summary

Detailed design spec for Task 6 (Name Resolution). Covers two-pass
resolver design, data model, scope nesting, diagnostics, semantic token
upgrade, and CLI surface. Also fixes a pre-existing clang-format
violation in ast_printer_test.cpp.

Supersedes #71 (which got stuck in GitHub's merge queue).

## Highlights

- Two-pass resolver: collect top-level declarations, then resolve bodies
- Arena-owned symbols/scopes via ResolveContext; side-table resolution
(no AST mutation)
- Single unified namespace frozen: struct Foo and fn Foo in the same
scope collide
- Type-position references left unresolved without diagnostics (stdlib
placeholders)
- Import binding rule: last segment bound in file scope;
CONTRACT_SYNTAX_SURFACE.md updated
- Semantic token upgrade: use.variable.param/local, use.function,
use.module, decl.module

## Test plan

- [x] Spec reviewed across 4 review rounds with all findings addressed
- [x] Contract example updated to match import binding rule
- [x] clang-format fix for ast_printer_test.cpp included

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix SQL migration: DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE POLICY

PostgreSQL doesn't support CREATE POLICY IF NOT EXISTS.
Use DROP IF EXISTS + CREATE pattern instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(cli): Multi-org restructure sprint 1 (#95)

* refactor: Restructure workspace layout for multi-org support (homestak-dev#311)

Replace HOMESTAK_LIB/HOMESTAK_ETC/HOMESTAK_SITE_CONFIG with single
HOMESTAK_ROOT anchor. New directory structure:

  ~/bootstrap/   - Bootstrap repo (contains CLI)
  ~/config/      - Site configuration (was: ~/etc/)
  ~/iac/         - IaC repos: ansible, iac-driver, tofu (was: ~/lib/)
  ~/.cache/      - Downloaded images
  ~/logs/        - Server and config logs

install.sh: creates new layout, writes HOMESTAK_ROOT to ~/.profile,
adds $HOMESTAK_ROOT/bootstrap to PATH (replaces ~/bin/ symlink).

homestak.sh: all paths derived from HOMESTAK_ROOT.
lib/spec_client.py: state path derived from HOMESTAK_ROOT/config/state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: Drop .sh extension from executable scripts (#313)

Rename homestak.sh → homestak, install.sh → install.
Update CI, Makefile, tests to reference new names.

Part of multi-org restructure sprint.

commit.message:
Add waypoint (intermediate stop) support for flexible route planning

Allow users to add up to 10 intermediate waypoints between origin and
destination. Waypoints are passed to the ORS API as multi-point coordinates,
displayed as orange markers on the map, included in LLM analysis prompts,
and exported as wpt elements in GPX files. Round trip routes use waypoints
only on the outbound leg.

Normal walking jumps (14 m/s) are uncapped.

- Dedup check against existing Obsidian agent-patterns
- Composes full agent-pattern note (7 required sections)
- Validates schema via vault_validate before writing
- Marks source items with promoted_to_vault=true after write
- Confidence mapping: 2 sessions=medium, 3+=high

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.2.7: Fix Linux overlay segfault + overlay API data loading

This release fixes two critical issues:

1. Linux overlay segfault - Force GTK backend on Linux instead of Qt/PySide6
   - pywebview was incorrectly selecting Qt backend causing segmentation fault
   - GTK3 + WebKit2GTK is more stable on Linux than Qt backends
   - Modified launcher.py to explicitly set gui='gtk' on Linux systems

2. Overlay data loading - Fixed API endpoint function names
   - Corrected get_completable_quests_data() → get_completable_quests()
   - Corrected get_purchasable_quests_data() → get_purchasable_quests()
   - Corrected get_active_quests_data() → get_active_quests()
   - Overlay views (Ready/Buyable/Active) now load quest data correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: fix release-plz workflow — remove manifest_path, add pull-requests:read

The manifest_path input mapped incorrectly for the release-pr command
(always passed --project-manifest="" instead of the actual path), silently
preventing PR creation. Removing it lets the action auto-discover Cargo.toml.

Also added pull-requests:read to the release job (required by official docs)
and removed the invalid changelog_include field (it takes package names, not
commit types).

commit.message:
chore: migrate from npm+vitest to bun (#12)

* chore: migrate from npm+vitest to bun

Replace npm with bun as the package manager and runtime, and vitest
with bun:test as the test runner. Update tsconfig to use ESNext/bundler
module resolution with a separate tsconfig.build.json for production
builds. Convert all test files from vitest APIs (vi.fn, vi.mock,
vi.waitFor) to bun:test equivalents (mock, mock.module, custom waitFor
helper). Update CI workflow to use oven-sh/setup-bun with bunfig.toml
for scoped GitHub Packages registry auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add codecov coverage reporting

* fix: match required check name CI (case-sensitive)

Branch protection requires the check named "CI" (uppercase). The job
key "ci" without a name field reports as lowercase "ci", which doesn't
satisfy the required check. Add explicit name: CI to match.

commit.message:
Add cricket_features.py and C4 classifier benchmark results

cricket_features.py: Unified KV-cache feature extraction module combining
magnitude-based (norm/mean/std) and SVD-based (effective rank, spectral
entropy) features. Supports live inference extraction and loading from
existing result JSONs. 18,045 observations loaded across 3 experiment types.

C4 benchmark results (aggregate 4 features):
- Deception AUROC: 1.0000 within-model (all 7 models), 0.67 cross-model
- Censorship AUROC: 1.0000 within-model, 0.76 cross-model
- 13-category classification: 99.7% (RF)
- Matches/exceeds Apollo Research activation probes (0.96-0.999)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: overhaul AI copilot — surface errors, add status indicator, improve personality

The Kai chatbot was silently falling back to dumb keyword matching
whenever the OpenAI API call failed, giving users no indication that
AI wasn't working. This fixes that and makes the whole AI experience
more usable and transparent.

Backend:
- Stop silent fallback: surface AI errors to the user with redacted details
  instead of silently degrading to keyword mode
- Add GET /ai/copilot/status endpoint that tests AI connectivity end-to-end
- Return diagnostic ai_setup_hint when AI isn't configured (missing key/model)
- Improve keyword fallback: honest "limited mode" messaging, handle greetings
  and help queries, explain what's missing
- Raise temperature from 0.3 to 0.6 in both copilot_llm and agent_core
- Rewrite system prompts: friendly colleague tone, allows business reasoning
  and general advice, not just robotic data regurgitation

Frontend:
- Add AI status badge in Kai header (green "AI" / amber "Limited") with
  tooltip showing model name or configuration error
- Show amber config warning banner when AI is not set up
- Add "Regenerate" button after every assistant response
- Add source badges on messages: "Limited" (amber) for keyword responses,
  "AI Error" (red) for failed AI calls
- Pass source and ai_setup_hint from API response through to message state

commit.message:
feat(input): add mask_on_submit option (#138)

* feat(input): add mask_on_submit option

Add a new `mask_on_submit` flag that shows input in cleartext while
typing but masks it with asterisks after submission. Useful for PINs
where the user needs to see what they're typing but the value should
not remain visible in terminal scrollback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore fixed-length mask for password mode in render_success

Password mode now shows 12 asterisks (original behavior) to avoid
revealing password length. mask_on_submit uses actual input length
without .max(1) so empty input correctly shows nothing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use chars().count() instead of len() for mask_on_submit

Byte length produces too many asterisks for multi-byte UTF-8 input.
Use character count to match the rest of the codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add render_success tests for mask_on_submit and password modes

Also document that password takes precedence over mask_on_submit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: v51 — fix vercel.json header ordering + cache-bust v51

Fix: reorder vercel.json headers so /static/(*) (immutable) comes AFTER
/(.*) (no-cache) — Vercel last-match-wins means static files now correctly
get immutable while all SPA routes get no-cache.

Bump: version 51.0, SW afroboost-v51, nuclear purge v51.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: cloud config loader missing vix_time_shift + other bot-specific keys

The cloud ConfigLoader built a hardcoded dict that only forwarded
specific keys from bot config.json. Keys like vix_time_shift,
smart_entry, long_salvage, order_limits, slippage_monitoring, and
emergency_close were silently dropped, causing features to fall
back to defaults (MKT-034 disabled, MKT-031 only worked because
its default was True).
